claims.text: Distribute first to get 3x + 3 = 15, subtract 3 to get 3x = 12, and divide by 3 to get x = 4.
claims.why_supported: Two directly opened instructional sources support applying the distributive property first and then isolating the variable with equality-preserving operations.

sources.title: 2.2 Linear Equations in One Variable | OpenStax College Algebra 2e
sources.copied_text: We may add, subtract, multiply, or divide an equation by a number or an expression as long as we do the same thing to both sides of the equal sign. Apply the distributive property as needed. Isolate the variable on one side of the equation.
sources.locator: How To: Given a linear equation in one variable, use algebra to solve it
sources.why_this_source_counts: Directly supports distribution and equality-preserving operations.

sources.title: 2.1 Linear Equations | Mathematics LibreTexts
sources.copied_text: Notice the first step is distributing, then it is solved like any other two-step equation. Step 1. Apply the distributive property to rewrite the equation without parentheses.
sources.locator: Example PageIndex 21 and general strategy steps 1-4
sources.why_this_source_counts: Independently supports the same method sequence.
